## [00:00 - 01:29]

What's your least favorite ad placement? So there is a point where we can over complicate it because if you optimize for a purchase or conversion of some sort, the algorithm sorts it out. Right. So there was a time where it was like, Oh, just use newsfeed, whatever. These other placements are very good. The problem is when you do that, you're forcing the algorithm to only use the most expensive placements. It's going to be too expensive or the likely if you're optimizing for a purchase, use all placements because more than likely certain placements won't be used anyway, because the algorithm is going to learn pretty quickly. It's not getting what, what you want out of it. What you got to watch out for is where there's weaknesses in certain placements. So for example, if you're optimizing for a link click or landing page view, audience network is the devil. Cause basically what that is is apps monetizing themselves with Facebook ads. And whether it's click fraud because they're trying to monetize themselves or accidental clicks because of the way some of those ads just pop up bots. Who knows? Whenever you get a refund from meta for your advertising 99 times out of 100, it's because of audience network, something that happened there. So when you optimize for a link click or landing page view, if all of a sudden you see results that are too good to be true because, oh, you're getting this amazing cost per click, break it down by placement. It's probably because of audience network by itself. That doesn't necessarily mean anything. Go to your, go to your results, like Google analytics and whatnot. What are they doing there? Probably a man, immediate abandon. You don't want that. That's not good.
